'''Freyyr's warblade''' was a powerful double-bladed [[Wookiee warblade]] owned by [[Freyyr]], a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] who served as the [[Wookiee Chieftain|chieftain]] of the [[City/Legends|village]] of [[Rwookrrorro/Legends|Rwookrrorro]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. In [[3956 BBY]], after being ousted from his position as chieftain by his son, [[Chuundar]], Freyyr used the warblade during his [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] to the [[Shadowlands/Legends|Shadowlands]] at the bottom of the forests of Kashyyyk. It was with this weapon that Freyyr attacked the amnesiac former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], and the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jolee Bindo]] when they were searching for a [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Map]] in the Shadowlands. At one point before the [[Dark Wars]], the [[Sword/Legends|sword]], which had Freyyr's symbol carved into it, was taken off the Wookiee [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Kashyyyk.

==Description==
[[Freyyr]]'s [[Wookiee warblade|warblade]] was a very powerful double-bladed melee weapon, upon which was inscribed the symbol of Freyyr, a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Wookiee Chieftain|chieftain]] of the [[City/Legends|village]] [[Rwookrrorro/Legends|Rwookrrorro]]. Like other Wookiee warblades, it required two [[Limb/Legends|hands]] for effective use. Its blades were [[Silver/Legends|silver]]-[[Color/Legends|colored]], and the handle of the weapon was a shade of brown.<ref name="kotor2" />

==History==
[[File:FreyyrInTheShadowlands-KOTOR.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Freyyr with his warblade in the Shadowlands]]
Freyyr's warblade was owned by the Wookiee chieftain Freyyr, who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] in the village of Rwookrrorro during the [[Jedi Civil War]].<ref name="kotor" /> In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="kotorcg" /> he took the double-bladed weapon with him when he was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] to the [[Shadowlands/Legends|Shadowlands]], the bottom of the forests of Kashyyyk, after being ousted from his position by his son, [[Chuundar]]. While in the Shadowlands, Freyyr became almost feral, to the extent that he would attack anyone he met, even his own friends. When the amnesiac former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] was searching for a [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Map]] on Kashyyyk, his search took him to the Shadowlands, where he obtained the aid of the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jolee Bindo]]. In the Shadowlands, Freyyr encountered Revan and Bindo, and due to Freyyr's savage behavior, he attacked them with his warblade in spite of his friendship with Bindo.<ref name="kotor" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
However, Revan and Bindo defeated Freyyr without [[Death/Legends|killing]] him, and they were able to reason with him and discovered the story of his exile.<ref name="kotor" />
{{Endgame}}

By the [[Dark Wars]],<ref name="kotorcg" /> Freyyr's warblade had been transported off of the Wookiee [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Kashyyyk and was no longer in Freyyr's possession. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], the weapon was valued at 29,955 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="kotor2" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:FreyyrRwookrrorro-KOTOR.jpg|thumb|right|140px|Freyyr's other sword]]
Freyyr's warblade made its first appearance in the [[2003]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. While Freyyr had the warblade during scenes in the Kashyyyk Shadowlands, the warblade was not the sword that Freyyr used later in the game when taking back his position as chieftain from his son, Chuundar. During the coup, Freyyr wielded a single-bladed weapon that was similar to the [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]] seen in the game.<ref name="kotor" /> The weapon was first referred to as Freyyr's warblade in the [[2004]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' by [[Obsidian Entertainment]]. In ''The Sith Lords'', it was a rare melee weapon that could be found in randomly determined places throughout the game. Due to this fact, the player was not guaranteed to find the weapon each time through the game. Freyyr's warblade was one of the most powerful melee weapons in the game, and it also possessed an increased attack bonus.<ref name="kotor2" />
